trajectory_summary: This is not a call — it is a voicemail intercept with no human interaction. The rep reached an automated voicemail system, and the transcript ends there. No qualification, no rapport, no appointment, no coaching signal from rep behavior is available. The only actionable question is whether the rep left a message, and the transcript provides no evidence either way.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as 'unknown,' which is accurate. There is no bookable outcome, no conversation, and no basis for any other classification. The call should be logged as a voicemail-intercept / no-contact attempt.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Voicemail protocol — does the rep have one and are they using it?
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
primary_coaching_focus.why: The transcript contains no rep speech. The manager cannot tell whether the rep left a message, hung up, or was cut off by the recording system. On a paid lead, every no-contact attempt without a structured voicemail is a missed recovery opportunity. The coaching conversation is not about this call's content — it is about whether this rep has a consistent voicemail discipline at all, and whether the floor has a standard for what to say when no one picks up.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
key_moments.stewart_read: The call never connected to a human. The transcript is entirely an automated voicemail greeting with no rep response captured. There is no strategic-vs-reactive distinction to make because there is no rep behavior to evaluate.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
key_moments.stewart_read: Lead type is unknown for this call. On a paid lead, a no-contact attempt still consumes a dial against a lead that requires a bill before the appointment can be set. If this is a paid lead and the rep is not leaving a structured callback message, the sit opportunity is leaking without any recovery mechanism.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
key_moments.stewart_read: No bill collection is possible on a voicemail intercept. If the rep's only follow-up path is a personal phone number or a manual callback, this lead is at risk of going cold with no systematic recovery. The schema's automated bill-capture roadmap item is directly relevant here — a structured SMS follow-up triggered from the dialer would give this lead a recovery path the rep cannot provide manually.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to voice mail. The person you're trying to reach is not available. At the tone, please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up.
key_moments.stewart_read: The transcript capture itself is the signal here. A recording that ends at the voicemail greeting — with no rep audio — may indicate a transcription cutoff, a rep who hung up immediately, or a dialer that dropped the call at the voicemail detection. Each has a different operational implication and none can be resolved from this transcript alone.
